description: KG-AUTO-2 knowledge-tier annotation of the directed TwoLaneHighway curated edges. This is an EXPERIMENT ANNOTATION over edges that already exist in parameter_relationships.json -- it adds NO graph edges and is never loaded by the validator/sync. It exists so LLM-extraction recall can be split by tier.

criterion.definitional: The dependency is stated in HCM narrative/prose -- a reader recovers it from the text without parsing a formula or table (e.g. 'the posted speed limit is used to estimate the base free-flow speed', 'demand exceeding capacity yields LOS F').
criterion.numeric: The dependency exists only inside an equation, an exhibit/threshold table, or a coefficient lookup -- a reader recovers it only by parsing that numeric artifact (e.g. the a_LS term in Eq. 15-4; the follower-density thresholds in Exhibit 15-6; alignment-class coefficient selection in Eqs. 15-8..15-25).
criterion.rater: single author rater; second-rater Cohen's kappa is a noted methodological strengthening (see kg_auto2_agreement.md), mirroring the Table 5 ablation plan.
criterion.note: Borderline cases are resolved by 'does Ch. 15 state the bare dependency in prose?' -- spl->ffs and volume/phf->flow_rate are narrated definitions and labelled definitional even though a formula also exists.

out_of_corpus.design_speed->ssd: AASHTO Green Book content; stopping sight distance is not derived in HCM Ch. 15, so this edge is unrecoverable from the indexed corpus by construction.
out_of_corpus.speed_limit->ssd: AASHTO-sourced; SSD not in HCM Ch. 15.
out_of_corpus.grade->ssd: AASHTO-sourced; SSD not in HCM Ch. 15.

out_of_corpus_note: These edges are excluded from the KG-AUTO-2 gold set (same exclusion principle applied to Ch. 12 / BasicFreeway): a 'read HCM Ch. 15' extractor cannot be scored against edges whose source authority is absent from the corpus. They remain labelled below for completeness.

justifications.design_speed->ssd: AASHTO/HCM narrate SSD as governed by design speed.
justifications.speed_limit->ssd: Sight-distance requirement narrated from posted/regulatory speed.
justifications.grade->ssd: Grade's effect on braking distance is stated in prose.
justifications.grade->vertical_class: Ch. 15 narrates vertical-class assignment from grade and length.
justifications.hor_class->spl: Alignment class narrated as affecting appropriate operating speed.
justifications.spl->ffs: Ch. 15 Step 3 prose: base FFS is estimated from the posted speed limit.
justifications.volume->flow_rate: Demand flow rate is the narrated definition v = V/PHF; dependency is stated.
justifications.phf->flow_rate: PHF is the defined peaking adjustment in the flow-rate definition.
justifications.capacity->los: Prose states demand exceeding capacity yields LOS F.
justifications.design_rad->hor_class: Horizontal class assigned from radius via an exhibit band table.
justifications.phv->avg_speed: Heavy-vehicle term enters the Eq. 15-7 coefficients.
justifications.followers_density->los: LOS assigned by follower-density thresholds, Exhibit 15-6.
justifications.avg_speed->los: Speed selects the follower-density threshold column, Exhibit 15-6.
justifications.passing_type->capacity: Capacity values by passing type, Exhibit 15-5.
justifications.vertical_class->capacity: Capacity reduction by vertical class, Exhibit 15-5.
